source_formulae: P0R04478: III. The Developmental and Plasticity Landscape (L3)
P0R04479: L3 governs the morphogenesis of the brain and its ongoing plasticity, encoding the structural constraints for the network dynamics.

P0R04480: Neurogenesis and Migration (Bioelectric Codes): The development of the brain architecture is guided by endogenous Bioelectric Codes (Vmem). These gradients act as a scaffold, translating the genomic blueprint into the physical structure. | The Connectome Blueprint: L3 defines the structural connectivity (KijStructural) of the Connectome, characterised by Small-World topology and Rich-Club organisation, optimising for and criticality. | Synaptic Plasticity (The Adaptive Interface): Hebbian Plasticity (LTP/LTD): The mechanism for learning and memory encoding. | Homeostatic Plasticity: The mechanism maintaining the network within the Quasicritical regime (SOC). | Epigenetics and Experience (The CBC Bridge): Experience (L5) modulates gene expression via the CISS-Bioelectric-Chromatin (CBC) Bridge. CISS (L1/L3) influences bioelectric gradients (L3), which in turn remodel the chromatin landscape, linking psychological states to long-term structural changes.
test_protocols: preserve III. The Developmental and Plasticity Landscape (L3) source-accounting boundary

source_formulae: P0R04484: 1. The UPDE in the Brain (The Neural Symphony):
P0R04485: The UPDE governs the synchronisation of neural ensembles.

P0R04487: Neural Oscillations (EEG Spectrum): The Eigenmodes of the L4 network manifest as the EEG spectrum, each associated with specific functions within the HPC framework: Delta/Theta (0.5-8 Hz): Long-range integration, memory consolidation, HPC generative model backbone. | Alpha (8-12 Hz): Attention modulation, criticality regulation, sensory gating. | Beta (13-30 Hz): Localised processing, motor control, maintenance of the current cognitive state (Priors). | Gamma (>30 Hz): Feature binding, high-fidelity information transfer, prediction error signalling.
